message.content: Hi there! Imagine you drive from city A to city B at 30 mph, and then you drive back from B to A at 60 mph. What do you think your average speed for the whole trip would be?
The average speed would be 45 mph because you just find the average of 30 and 60.
That is a super common guess, but it's actually not 45 mph! Think about this: do you spend the same amount of *time* driving at 30 mph as you do at 60 mph?
If the distance between the cities doesn't change, wouldn't the time you spend driving be the same both ways too?
Actually, if you drive faster, it takes you less time to cover that same distance, right? So, which leg of the trip do you end up spending more time on?
I'm confused because if the distance is the same, why wouldn't the time be the same too?
Think about walking versus running to your friend's house. Even though the distance to their house doesn't change, wouldn't it take you less time if you ran instead of walked?
I get that running is faster, but I'm just really stuck on how we can figure out the average speed if we don't even know the distance between the cities.

That is a really great question, and it's actually the key to solving this! Let's just make up an easy number for the distance—say, 60 miles—and see what happens. How long would the trip take each way if the distance was 60 miles?
If you drive faster at 60 mph, does that mean you actually burn more gas than when you were going 30 mph?

That is a cool real-world question, and usually yes, you do burn more gas at higher speeds because of wind resistance! But let's stick to our time puzzle for a second: if the distance is 60 miles, how many hours does it take to go each way?
It takes exactly one hour to go back at 60 mph, but I am still confused about how to calculate the slow part.
You got the fast part right, that's exactly one hour! For the slow part, if you are traveling 30 miles every hour, how long will it take you to cover that 60-mile distance?
It would take two hours.
Spot on! It takes two hours. So, for the whole round trip, what was your total distance traveled, and how many total hours did it take you?
The total distance is 120 miles, and it takes 3 hours in total.
Exactly! So if you went a total of 120 miles and it took you 3 hours, what was your average speed for the whole trip?
It would be 40 mph because 120 miles divided by 3 hours is 40.

Perfect! You got it, the average speed is 40 mph. Notice how it's closer to 30 than 60 because you spent twice as much time driving at that slower speed. 

To make sure this really clicked, what do you think would happen to the average speed if we chose a different distance, like 120 miles instead of 60?
It would still be 40 mph because the time would just double too, so the ratio stays the same.
